zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Strother於1303年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德時，她的父親William de Strother38歲，母親Joan Corbet35歲。她和Thomas de Blankenship至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1382年之前在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德卡素。

nameMeanings.name: Strother
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place somewhere in northeastern England formerly called (The) Strother, named with Middle English strother, struther, stroder ‘wooded marshland, marsh covered in brushwood’ (Old English strōther). This topographic term was current in Durham, Northumberland, and southern Scotland, but the place which gave rise to the surname has not been certainly identified. A locality called le strother is recorded near the city of Durham c. 1299, while another, recorded as le estrother in 1153–95, may have named Strother House in Boldon (Durham), unless the house was named from the surname. William de Strother was a tenant in nearby Offerton, in 1473, and he was probably a member of the Newcastle merchant-cum-gentry family from whom all or most modern bearers inherit their surname. In Northumberland, possibilities include Cold Strother in Kirkheaton and Haughton Strother, although there is no evidence for either place being known simply as The Strother. Alternatively, The Strother may have been a lost district name for either the marshy area around Cold Strother (north of the North Tyne) or that around Haughton Strother (on the south bank of the North Tyne), but again evidence is lacking. The (del) Strother family owned estates near (but not in) both places from at least the late 14th century. In southern Scotland, Struther in Lanarkshire and Struthers in Ayrshire and Fife (from the Older Scots equivalent of Middle English strother) might be considered, but for lack of supporting evidence it has been suggested that Scottish Strother is probably the attested Northumberland surname, whose bearers were major landholders on the Scottish border.
History: A William Strother is recorded in VA in 1673, probably the same man who, as William Strouder, was granted land in 1658.

variants.name: Struth
meanings: Scottish: topographic name from Older Scots stroth(e) ‘marshy place’. Compare Strother , Struthers .

variants.name: Struthers
meanings: English and Scottish (Lanarkshire): probably a variant of Strother , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Strother was born in 1303, in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, her father, William de Strother, was 38 and her mother, Joan Corbet, was 35. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Thomas de Blankenship. She died before 1382, in Castle, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Strother est née en 1303, à Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Strother, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Joan Corbet, avait 35 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Thomas de Blankenship. Elle est décédée avant 1382, à Castle, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Strother 1303 in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Strother 38 und ihre Mutter Joan Corbet 35. it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Strother è nata il nel 1303 nel Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William de Strother, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Joan Corbet, aveva 35 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Thomas de Blankenship. Morì prima del 1382, nel Castle, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Strother(이)가 1303년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Strother(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Corbet(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 Thomas de Blankenship(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1382년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 카스틀에서 사망했다.
